metadata.name: Terraces 01
metadata.description: Reimagined Victorian terraces made for contemporary living with generous spaces and a private courtyard in the centre of each home. The design provides light, air and greenery through internal courtyards while maintaining durable brick streetscapes.
designConcepts: Internal courtyard brings light into living space, stairwell and kitchen
Living areas directly connected to private rear garden
Spacious family bathroom on first floor
Brick detailing for refined street presentation
Skylights and courtyard allow natural light and ventilation
Provision for solar panels with large roof area

applicablePolicies.name: Pattern Book Development Code 2025
applicablePolicies.description: Pattern book development under State Environmental Planning Policy (Exempt and Complying Development Codes) 2008

excludedLand.description: Certain flood-prone land in high risk locations

slopeConstraints.notes: Pattern provides adaptations for sites with slopes up to 2.0m front-to-back and 1.5m side-to-side

orientationGuidance.notes: Courtyards, skylights and flexible street-facing living spaces optimise solar access
North-facing rear yards provide optimal solar access
Optional retractable solar blinds can shade east orientation
Optional retractable solar blinds can shade west orientation

lmr.notes: Third storey (attic/roof terrace) available in LMR areas only

height.notes: Maximum building height allows for adaptation to slope on subject site

lmr.notes: Primary street frontage setback in LMR areas

nonLmr.alternativeRule: Not less than average setback from primary road of nearest 2 dwelling houses within 40m on same side, whichever is greater
nonLmr.notes: Primary street frontage setback in non-LMR areas

partialSetback.notes: 0m min partial side setback for front 8.1m of building

nonLmr.notes: Side boundary setback in non-LMR areas

secondary.notes: Secondary street setback for corner lots

lmr.notes: Maximum 0.9:1 when selecting all adaptations and options; base pattern is 0.8:1

nonLmr.notes: Maximum 0.8:1 when selecting all adaptations and options; base pattern is 0.79:1

landscapeRequirements.guideReference: NSW Housing Pattern Book Landscape design guide
landscapeRequirements.treeCanopyGuide: NSW Government Tree Canopy Guide for Low and Mid Rise Housing

livingRooms.notes: Window more than 3m from boundary to living room must receive more than 3 hours direct sunlight

neighbouringProperties.notes: If window currently receives less than 3 hours, direct sunlight is not reduced

nonLmr.notes: Minimum 1 car parking space per dwelling

parking.notes: Max 2 out of 3 dwellings may incorporate a garage for terrace patterns. Parking alternatively provided as hardstand in non-LMR areas.

policyAreaVariants.notes: Includes attic adaptation with extra bedroom or roof terrace

parkingVariants.name: Front Garage
parkingVariants.description: Integrated garage at front of dwelling
parkingVariants.notes: End terraces can include garage; middle terrace uses on-street parking in LMR

parkingVariants.name: Rear Laneway Garage
parkingVariants.notes: 5m minimum clearance must be maintained between terraces and outbuildings

parkingVariants.name: Hardstand Space
parkingVariants.description: Car space provided as hardstand in front setback
parkingVariants.notes: Parking provided as hardstand in non-LMR housing areas

groundFloorVariants.name: Garage Option
groundFloorVariants.description: Enclosed garage at ground floor front

groundFloorVariants.name: Flexible Room Option
groundFloorVariants.description: Flexible room and bathroom in lieu of garage
groundFloorVariants.notes: Can be converted to guest room or granny flat

firstFloorVariants.name: Large Bedroom
firstFloorVariants.description: Larger bedroom configuration on first floor

firstFloorVariants.name: Bedroom and Study Split
firstFloorVariants.description: Separate bedroom and study on first floor

thirdFloorVariants.name: Roof Terrace
thirdFloorVariants.description: Private roof terrace in attic space
thirdFloorVariants.notes: Available only in LMR housing areas

thirdFloorVariants.name: Additional Living Room
thirdFloorVariants.notes: Available only in LMR housing areas

thirdFloorVariants.name: Additional Bedroom
thirdFloorVariants.notes: Available only in LMR housing areas

slopeVariants.name: Slope Front to Back
slopeVariants.notes: Internal stairs and adjustments accommodate up to 2.0m front to back

slopeVariants.name: Crossfall Site
slopeVariants.notes: Dwellings at different levels to accommodate up to 1.5m side to side

primaryCladding.detailing: Brick detailing for refined street presentation

additionalFeatures.name: Window Shading Device
additionalFeatures.description: External retractable blind for west/east facing windows
additionalFeatures.notes: Can mitigate heat loads when facades face east or west

additionalFeatures.name: Rear Studio
additionalFeatures.description: Detached studio or garage at rear of site
additionalFeatures.notes: When site length or access conditions permit

fixed.name: Party Wall Locations
fixed.description: Party walls between terraces are fixed

fixed.name: Building Footprint
fixed.description: Overall building footprint and setbacks are fixed

fixed.name: Internal Courtyard
fixed.reason: Light and ventilation to central rooms

fixed.name: Internal Stair Location
fixed.description: Position of internal staircase is fixed

fixed.name: Wet Area Locations
fixed.description: Kitchen and bathroom locations are fixed

flexible.name: Material Palette
flexible.description: Choice of four material palette options
constraints: Must select complete palette, not mix materials

flexible.name: Ground Floor Configuration
flexible.description: Choice between garage, flexible room or bedroom
constraints: End terraces only for garage option

flexible.name: First Floor Bedroom Layout
flexible.description: Choice between large bedroom or bedroom/study split

flexible.name: Attic Adaptation
flexible.description: Choice of roof terrace, additional living or additional bedroom

flexible.name: Window Shading
flexible.description: Optional retractable solar blinds for east/west orientations

flexible.name: Rear Laneway Garage
flexible.description: Optional rear garage for laneway sites

waste.notes: Some councils may allow smaller 120L bins; needs to be confirmed for local area

drivewayRequirements.notes: Driveways located min 6m from intersections; confirm with local authorities if additional rules apply

pedestrianSightLines.notes: Sight line must fit wholly on site or fencing kept below 800mm

photovoltaics.notes: Large roof area provision for solar panels

evCharging.notes: 15A outlet required in garage for potential EV charging

meterBoards.notes: Side gates should be located so meter panels can be accessed from outside

waterMeters.notes: Per dwelling in accordance with water authority requirements

stormwater.notes: Design is site specific; suitably qualified engineer required

hotWater.notes: All-electric homes with heat pump hot water units

ceilingFans.notes: Improve air circulation, energy efficiency and thermal comfort

airConditioning.notes: Pattern applicants will need to assess requirements

passiveDesign: Passive solar design ensures comfortable living
Daylight and cross-ventilation to all spaces
Sun shading and rain protection to windows and doors
Careful control of extent of glass maximises light and thermal comfort
constructionEfficiency: Simple construction system suited to prefabrication
Standardised elements reduce cost and construction waste
materialDurability: External materials including brickwork have longevity
energyEfficiency: All-electric homes provide healthier indoor environment
Lower energy bills and reduced carbon footprint

basixCompliance.notes: Each pattern includes guidance on achieving BASIX requirements

householdSuitability.description: Private garden visible from living area; spacious eat-in kitchen great for kids
Living areas separated from bedrooms to provide teen retreat
Flexible room on ground floor can be converted to guest room or granny flat
Designed to meet ABCB Livable Housing Design Standard to support changing mobility needs
Separation between bedrooms and living areas suits share housing arrangements

livableHousingStandard.notes: Complies with ABCB Livable Housing Design Standard adapted from LHA silver level

pathsOfTravel.notes: Driveways must be step free per ABCB Livable Housing Design Standard
